destinations.evidence: Shared base validation enforces executor/TTL bounds, 93-byte 0x04-prefixed ECIES blobs, and optional 65-byte 0x04-prefixed user keys.
The metadata router applies the shared checks to ExecutorRequest-derived payloads, DKMS, FHE, and sovereign-agent validation paths.
destinations.snippet:
HTTP_CALL_PRECOMPILE => validate_http_call_payload(...)
LLM_CALL_PRECOMPILE => validate_llm_call_payload(...)
FHE_PRECOMPILE => validate_fhe_call_payload(...)
DKMS_KEY_PRECOMPILE => validate_dkms_key_payload(...)
SOVEREIGN_AGENT_PRECOMPILE => validate_sovereign_agent_payload(...)

equivalence_argument: The port centralizes the crypto-shape checks in validate_executor_base_fields and direct sovereign-agent single-blob validation; empty optional fields pass, while non-empty malformed ECIES/user-key values fail before RPC admission.
notes: FHE storage credentials are intentionally excluded from ECIES validation; the base encrypted_secrets/user_public_key fields still use the shared checks.
rationale: The commit message says ECIES and user_public_key validation was added to all async precompiles through a shared validate_base_fields helper. The helper validates executor/ttl, every encrypted secret blob, and user_public_key format, and individual validators now route through it. The primary diff adds validate_base_fields, validate_ecies_blob, validate_ecies_blob_array, validate_user_public_key, new error variants, and routes HTTP, LLM, ExecutorRequest-derived payloads, DKMS, and other async validators through those shared checks. Collapsed 2 closely related historical candidate statements into the latest effective formulation.
statement: Async payload validation should reject non-empty encrypted_secrets entries shorter than 93 bytes or not starting with 0x04, and should reject non-empty user_public_key values unless they are exactly 65 bytes with 0x04 prefix; these base checks should be applied across ExecutorRequest-based async precompiles and DKMS-style requests in addition to executor and TTL checks.
suggested_falsification_tests: For each async payload type using ExecutorRequest base fields, submit payloads with encrypted_secrets empty, valid 93+ byte 0x04-prefixed ECIES blob, 92-byte blob, 0x03-prefixed blob, empty user_public_key, valid 65-byte 0x04-prefixed key, and invalid 64-byte or non-0x04 key. RPC validation must accept only the valid/empty cases and map errors to the new structured RPC variants.

For representative async precompiles, submit payloads with a non-empty encrypted secret shorter than 93 bytes, a secret not starting with 0x04, a 64-byte user_public_key, and a 65-byte key not starting with 0x04; assert validation returns the corresponding errors. Then submit empty optional fields and well-formed 0x04-prefixed values and assert base validation passes.
summary: Async payload validation enforces ECIES and user public key shapes.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For representative async payload types, submit non-empty ECIES blobs shorter than 93 bytes, blobs without 0x04, invalid 64-byte user keys, and valid/empty controls; assert only valid or empty cases pass.
title: Async payloads validate ECIES and user public key format
